chunks.text: Leon O. Chua is Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley's EECS department. His contact information includes office at 564 Cory Hall, phone 510-642-3209, email chua@eecs.berkeley.edu, and office hours on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 11:00-12:00 pm. He has a personal homepage, publications list, dissertations, and research support available through Thomas Parsons (tparsons@berkeley.edu).
chunks.question: What is Leon Chua's position and contact details?
chunks.answer: Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley EECS.

chunks.text: Leon Chua began his career as an Assistant Professor of Electrical Engineering at Purdue University in 1964, becoming Associate Professor in 1967, and joined UC Berkeley in 1970. He received the 2005 Gustav Kirchhoff Award, IEEE's highest technical field award for electronic circuits, and the 2000 IEEE Neural Networks Pioneer Award. Elected IEEE Fellow in 1974, he has won numerous prizes including the IEEE Browder J. Thompson Memorial Prize (1972), IEEE W. R. G. Baker Prize (1978), Frederick Emmons Award (1974), and M. E. Van Valkenhburg Award (1995, 1998). He holds 7 USA patents, 8 honorary doctorates, and was a top 15 cited engineering author in 2002. Elected to the European Academy of Sciences in 1997.
chunks.question: What are key milestones in Leon Chua's career and awards?
chunks.answer: Joined UC Berkeley in 1970, received Gustav Kirchhoff Award.

chunks.text: Leon Chua earned a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from the University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign in 1964, an M.S. from MIT in 1961, and a B.S. from Mapúa Institute of Technology, Manila, Philippines in 1959. His research areas include Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), focusing on cellular neural networks, cellular automata, complexity, nanoelectronics, nonlinear circuits and systems, nonlinear dynamics, and chaos.
chunks.question: What are Leon Chua's educational background and research interests?
chunks.answer: Ph.D. from University of Illinois, research in neural networks.

chunks.text: Selected publications by Leon Chua include works on continuous piecewise linear functions (2006), local activity and complexity (2005), Viterbi decoders with CNN arrays (2005), maximum likelihood decoding with CNN circuits (2005), nonlinear pattern classification (2005), hysteresis CNN (2004), perspectives on Wolfram's New Kind of Science (2004), cellular neural networks with resonant tunneling diodes (2004), structurally stable CNN (2004), star CNN for memories (2004), time-delayed impulsive control (2004), CNN genes design (2003), universal neuron perspective (2003), CNN Universal Machine review (2003), target tracking with CNN-UM (2003), and books on cellular neural networks and visual computing (2002), CNN paradigm (1998), cellular neural networks theory (1988), double scroll family (1986), and nonlinear network theory (1969).
chunks.question: What topics do Leon Chua's selected publications cover?
chunks.answer: Cellular neural networks, nonlinear dynamics, and complexity.

chunks.text: Leon Chua has received numerous honors and awards, including the Julius Springer Prize for Applied Physics (2020), IEEE EDS Celebrated Member Award (2019), Guggenheim Fellow (2010), IEEE CASS Vitold Belevitch Circuits and Systems Award (2007), IEEE Gustav Robert Kirchhoff Award (2005), IEEE CASS 2000 Millennium Medal (2000), CIS Neural Networks Pioneer Award (2000), IEEE CASS Mac E. Van Valkenburg Award (1998, 1995), IEEE CASS Charles A. Desoer Technical Achievement Award (1993), IEEE CASS Guillemin-Cauer Best Paper Award (1989, 1985, 1972), IEEE Fellow (1974), Frederick Emmons Terman Award (1974), IEEE W. R. G. Baker Prize Paper Award (1973), and IEEE Browder J. Thompson Memorial Prize Paper Award (1967).
chunks.question: What recent and notable awards has Leon Chua received?
chunks.answer: Julius Springer Prize 2020, IEEE EDS Award 2019.
